what_it_does: Batch-extracts structured JSON summaries from Markdown files in a local project tree using a local LLM endpoint, writing results as JSONL and resuming from prior runs.
observations: Hardcodes ROOT to a Windows/WSL path for a 'Visual Designer' project and OUT to ~/wizard-run-1/extract.jsonl.
Uses a local v1/chat/completions endpoint at localhost:8000 with model cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit.
System prompt asks for a strict JSON schema capturing what, intent, components, decisions, open_questions, gaps_or_contradictions, latent_or_abandoned.
Implements retry logic: the first two attempts request response_format json_object, later attempts fall back to plain generation.
Reads up to 115000 characters per file and skips files with fewer than 40 non-whitespace characters.
Deduplicates against prior successful extractions in the output file and rewrites it to drop errored records.
Uses an 8-thread ThreadPoolExecutor and a bash find subprocess to enumerate Markdown files excluding node_modules and .git.
standouts: The system prompt is tuned for messy, half-designed AI-generated documents and explicitly asks for latent/abandoned ideas.
Resume behavior rewrites the existing JSONL to keep only good-done records, which is unusual but file-local.
Guided JSON attempts are toggled by the guided parameter rather than always being enabled.
conventions: Uses the standard library plus subprocess for file discovery.
JSONL append-only output with in-place resume filtering.
Simple imperative script style with global constants and inline error handling.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute paths and model name reduce portability.
The subprocess shell command injects ROOT without escaping, which is risky if ROOT contains single quotes.
No rate limiting or backpressure beyond 8 workers and a 240-second timeout.
Resume logic loads all prior output into memory and rewrites the file, which could be slow for large runs.
On unguided attempts the model may emit non-JSON that fails json.loads and is recorded as an error.
notes: A one-off ingestion harness that uses a local LLM to turn an unruly Markdown design corpus into a structured JSONL ledger, with resume and parallel extraction.
questions: Is the 115000-character read limit chosen to fit the local model's context window?
Why are only the first two attempts guided instead of all attempts?
What is the intended downstream consumer of extract.jsonl?
Does the deduplication logic intentionally discard records that previously errored?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (batch-extract structured summaries from scattered Markdown files) matches the implementation; the resume/dedup behavior is an extra convenience not mentioned in the prompt.
intention_signals: It tries to turn a disorganized, possibly abandoned AI-generated design corpus into a machine-readable audit trail of ideas, decisions, gaps, and unfinished concepts.
novelty: The extraction schema explicitly includes 'latent_or_abandoned' and 'gaps_or_contradictions', treating the corpus as an archaeological site rather than a clean spec.
contribution: Provides a concrete extraction schema and a runnable, resumable batch harness for summarizing Markdown design artifacts with a local LLM.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that parallel-calls a local chat-completions LLM to extract structured JSON summaries from Markdown files in a project tree, writing JSONL output and resuming from previous runs.
intention_for_embedding: To audit and catalog a scattered, AI-generated design corpus by surfacing each file's intent, components, decisions, open questions, contradictions, and abandoned ideas.

symbols.does: Sends a chat-completions request to the local LLM endpoint with the system prompt and optional JSON response format, returning the model's content.
symbols.description: Builds a payload including the system prompt, user content, max_tokens, and temperature; if guided is true it requests a json_object response format. It then POSTs to URL and returns the message content string. This is the low-level LLM invocation used by extract.

symbols.does: Reads a Markdown file, packages its relative path and content for the LLM, retries up to four times, and returns a structured JSON record or an error record.
symbols.description: Opens the file with a 115000-character cap and ignores near-empty files. It constructs a payload prefixed with PATH, calls the LLM with guided JSON on the first two attempts, parses the result, injects the relative path, and handles HTTP and parse errors across retries. It is mapped over the file list by the main thread pool.
